email_drafting_system.description: AI-powered escalation email drafting system for Returnkart. Generates professional return/refund complaint emails based on order context and issue type.

escalation_levels.name: Initial Return Request
escalation_levels.description: Polite first request to initiate return or report issue
escalation_levels.when_to_use: First contact. Return window is still open. No prior complaint.

escalation_levels.name: Follow-Up Escalation
escalation_levels.description: Firmer follow-up when initial request was ignored or denied unfairly
escalation_levels.when_to_use: No response within 48 hours, or return request rejected without valid reason.

escalation_levels.name: Senior Escalation / Grievance
escalation_levels.description: Formal escalation to senior management or grievance officer
escalation_levels.when_to_use: Multiple ignored requests, consumer rights violation, or significant financial loss.

escalation_levels.name: Consumer Forum / Legal Notice
escalation_levels.description: Final notice before filing complaint with Consumer Forum or CCPA
escalation_levels.when_to_use: All internal escalations exhausted. Ready to file legal complaint.

issue_types.description: Product arrived physically damaged, broken, or non-functional
issue_types.consumer_right_reference: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - Section 2(6): Deficiency in service

issue_types.description: Product received is different from what was ordered
evidence_needed: Photos of received product vs ordered product
issue_types.consumer_right_reference: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - Section 2(6): Deficiency in service; Section 2(28): Misleading advertisement if listing was incorrect

issue_types.description: Return was picked up but refund hasn't been processed within promised timeline
evidence_needed: Tracking showing item received by seller
issue_types.consumer_right_reference: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - Section 2(6): Deficiency in service; RBI guidelines on refund timelines

issue_types.description: Courier marked as delivered but customer never received the package
evidence_needed: GPS location proof at delivery time
issue_types.consumer_right_reference: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - Section 2(6): Deficiency in service; IPC Section 420: Cheating

issue_types.description: Platform rejected a valid return request within the return window
issue_types.consumer_right_reference: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - Section 2(6): Deficiency in service; E-commerce Rules 2020

issue_types.description: Return approved but no pickup has been arranged within promised time
evidence_needed: Proof that pickup window has passed
issue_types.consumer_right_reference: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - Section 2(6): Deficiency in service

issue_types.description: Refund amount is less than what was paid for the product
evidence_needed: Return policy showing full refund eligibility
issue_types.consumer_right_reference: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - Section 2(6): Deficiency in service

issue_types.display_name: Product Quality Does Not Match Listing
issue_types.description: Product received is of significantly lower quality than advertised
issue_types.consumer_right_reference: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - Section 2(28): Misleading advertisement; Section 2(47): Unfair trade practice

issue_types.display_name: Return Window Expired Due to Platform Delay
issue_types.description: Return window expired because the platform took too long to respond to initial complaint
issue_types.consumer_right_reference: Consumer Protection Act 2019 - Section 2(6): Deficiency in service; Platform's own SLA violation

level_2.subject_template: URGENT: Follow-Up on Unresolved Return - Order #{order_id}

level_3.subject_template: FORMAL GRIEVANCE: Unresolved Consumer Complaint - Order #{order_id} - Escalation to Grievance Officer

level_4.subject_template: LEGAL NOTICE: Violation of Consumer Rights - Order #{order_id} - Final Notice Before Legal Action
level_4.body_template:
LEGAL NOTICE

To,
{platform_legal_name}
Registered Office: {platform_address}

From,
{user_name}
{user_address}

Date: {current_date}

Subject: Legal Notice under Consumer Protection Act, 2019 for Deficiency in Service and Unfair Trade Practice

Dear Sir/Madam,

Under instructions from my client / I, the undersigned, do hereby issue this legal notice upon you as follows:

1. FACTS OF THE CASE:
   My client / I placed an order bearing Order ID {order_id} on your platform on {order_date} for "{item_name}" amounting to ₹{amount}.

2. ISSUE:
   {detailed_issue_description}

3. FAILED ATTEMPTS AT RESOLUTION:
   {complaint_timeline}

4. LEGAL PROVISIONS VIOLATED:
   a) Consumer Protection Act, 2019 - Section 2(6): Deficiency in Service
   b) Consumer Protection Act, 2019 - Section 2(47): Unfair Trade Practice
   c) Consumer Protection (E-Commerce) Rules, 2020 - Rule 4(4) and 4(5)
   d) {additional_legal_reference}

5. DEMAND:
   I hereby demand that you:
   a) Process the complete {return_type} of ₹{amount} within 7 days
   b) Pay compensation of ₹{compensation_amount} for mental agony, inconvenience, and harassment
   c) Pay the cost of this legal notice

6. CONSEQUENCE OF NON-COMPLIANCE:
   If the above demands are not met within 15 days of receipt of this notice, I shall be constrained to initiate legal proceedings before the appropriate Consumer Disputes Redressal Forum/Commission, where you shall be liable for:
   - Refund of the full amount
   - Compensation for mental agony and harassment
   - Cost of litigation
   - Any other relief the Hon'ble Forum/Commission deems fit

You are hereby called upon to comply with the above demands to avoid further legal consequences.

{user_name}
{user_address}
Date: {current_date}

email_drafting_system.gemini_drafting_prompt:
You are a consumer rights email drafting assistant for Returnkart, an Indian e-commerce return tracking app.

Given the following order context and issue details, draft a professional escalation email.

RULES:
1. Use the appropriate escalation level template as a base
2. Personalize with actual order details, dates, and amounts
3. Reference the correct return policy for the platform and category
4. Include relevant sections of Consumer Protection Act 2019 and E-Commerce Rules 2020
5. Be factual and professional - no emotional language
6. Include a clear timeline of events if this is a follow-up
7. Always include evidence references if available
8. Calculate and include the correct return window expiry date
9. Use Indian English spelling and conventions
10. Keep the tone appropriate to the escalation level

INPUT FORMAT:
```json
{
  "escalation_level": 1-4,
  "order_id": "string",
  "platform": "string",
  "item_name": "string",
  "category": "string",
  "amount": number,
  "order_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "delivery_date": "YYYY-MM-DD",
  "issue_type": "damaged_product | wrong_product | refund_delayed | ghost_delivery | return_rejected | pickup_not_scheduled | partial_refund | quality_issue | expired_window_dispute",
  "issue_description": "string",
  "evidence_available": ["list of evidence types"],
  "previous_complaints": [{"date": "YYYY-MM-DD", "channel": "string", "response": "string"}],
  "user_name": "string",
  "user_email": "string",
  "user_phone": "string"
}
```

OUTPUT: Return the complete email with subject line and body, ready to send.
